message:
cli,docs: Require login hostname and remove dev auth mode

🔮 View transcript: https://agentlogs.ai/s/klqbaic4s88ogadgh49mrp36

files.patch:
@@ -113,13 +113,13 @@ tail -f logs/dev.log
 bun agentlogs status
 
 # Re-authenticate
-bun agentlogs login
+bun agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
 ```
 
 ## CLI Usage
 
 ```bash
-bun agentlogs login                              # Authenticate
+bun agentlogs login agentlogs.ai                 # Authenticate
 bun agentlogs status                             # Check login status
 bun agentlogs claudecode upload transcript.jsonl # Upload transcript
 bun agentlogs claudecode sync                    # Sync all local transcripts

@@ -3,6 +3,20 @@ title: "Changelog"
 description: "Product updates and announcements"
 ---
 
+<Update label="February 8, 2026">
+## CLI Login Hostname Required
+
+`agentlogs login` now requires an explicit hostname:
+
+```bash
+npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
+```
+
+- Removed `--dev` special-casing from authentication commands
+- You can now authenticate against any host by passing it directly
+- `logout` accepts an optional hostname and logs out from all hosts when omitted
+</Update>
+
 <Update label="February 4, 2025">
 ## Pi Support

@@ -12,19 +12,29 @@ description: Complete CLI command reference
 Authenticate with AgentLogs using device authorization.
 
 ```bash
-npx agentlogs login
+npx agentlogs login <hostname>
 ```
 
-Opens a browser for authentication. After authorizing, your token is stored locally.
+Examples:
+
+```bash
+npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
+npx agentlogs login localhost:3000
+```
+
+The hostname is required. The CLI opens a browser for authentication and stores your token locally for that host.
 
 ### logout
 
 Remove stored authentication credentials.
 
 ```bash
-npx agentlogs logout
+npx agentlogs logout [hostname]
 ```
 
+- With a hostname, logs out only from that host.
+- Without a hostname, logs out from all configured hosts.
+
 ### status
 
 Show current authentication status and account info.
@@ -33,12 +43,15 @@ Show current authentication status and account info.
 npx agentlogs status
 ```
 
-**Output:**
+**Output (example):**
 
 ```
-Logged in as: user@example.com
-Environment: production
-Synced transcripts: 42
+🔐 AgentLogs Authentication Status
+
+agentlogs.ai (https://agentlogs.ai)
+  ✅ Logged in
+  👤 Example User
+  📧 user@example.com
 ```

@@ -399,18 +412,14 @@ npx agentlogs deny /path/to/secret-repo
 
 These options work with any command:
 
-| Option        | Description                                    |
-| ------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
-| `--help`      | Show help for a command                        |
-| `--version`   | Show CLI version                               |
-| `--env <env>` | Use environment: `production` or `development` |
+| Option      | Description             |
+| ----------- | ----------------------- |
+| `--help`    | Show help for a command |
+| `--version` | Show CLI version        |
 
 **Example:**
 
 ```bash
 # Get help for a specific command
 npx agentlogs claudecode sync --help
-
-# Use development environment
-npx agentlogs --env development claudecode sync
 ```

@@ -17,7 +17,13 @@ The AgentLogs CLI is the primary way to sync transcripts from AI coding assistan
 Before syncing, you need to log in to your AgentLogs account:
 
 ```bash
-npx agentlogs login
+npx agentlogs login <hostname>
+```
+
+For production:
+
+```bash
+npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
 ```
 
 This initiates a device authorization flow:
@@ -35,16 +41,19 @@ Verify you're logged in:
 npx agentlogs status
 ```
 
-This shows:
+This shows, per configured host:
 
-- Your logged-in email
-- Current environment (production/development)
-- Number of synced transcripts
+- Authentication status
+- Logged-in user details
+- Last login time
 
 ## Logout
 
 To log out and remove stored credentials:
 
 ```bash
-npx agentlogs logout
+npx agentlogs logout [hostname]
 ```
+
+- With hostname: logs out from that host only
+- Without hostname: logs out from all hosts

 # Authenticate
-npx agentlogs login
+npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
 npx agentlogs status
 npx agentlogs logout
